title: Transformer Benchmark on Indonesian Reviews
excerpt: A small, honest benchmark of two transformer families across the metrics that actually matter in production.
content:
## Setup

We trained several transformer-based sentiment classifiers on 180k Indonesian product reviews, split 80/10/10. Each model used identical preprocessing and the same five-fold evaluation harness.

## Results

- **Macro F1:** best transformer model +4.1 points vs baseline
- **p95 latency:** optimized transformer serving at 220ms vs 310ms baseline on an A10
- **Memory:** optimized transformer setup used ~22% less peak VRAM

## Caveats

Results are dataset-specific. Indonesian morphology and domain vocabulary can change model rankings significantly.

## Takeaway

When latency budgets are tight and the language is morphology-rich, transformer selection and serving strategy matter as much as raw model size.
seoDescription: A practical comparison of transformer models on Indonesian sentiment data.

title: Laravel Queues, In Anger
excerpt: Field-tested patterns for Horizon, retries, and idempotency in production Laravel.
content:
## Why queues?

Latency is a feature. Move anything that isn't essential to the request out.

## Patterns

1. **Idempotency keys** for every job that can be retried.
2. **Dead-letter buckets** rather than swallowing failures.
3. **Separate queues** for short, long, and bursty jobs.

## Observability

Horizon is great for the green path. For everything else, log structured events and stream to your favorite OLAP store.

title: Shipping Fast Next.js Apps
excerpt: Three patterns I keep reaching for: route handlers, partial prerendering, and a strict image budget.
content:
## Route handlers > pages/api

For most endpoints, App Router's route handlers are simpler and more composable.

## Pre-render the obvious

Marketing pages don't need to be dynamic. ISR them; you'll thank yourself later.

## Image budget

Set a per-page image budget in KB. Reject any commit that breaks it.
